strengths: 15+ years of progressive full-stack experience spanning Node.js, TypeScript, React, and cloud/container infrastructure (AWS, Kubernetes, Docker), directly matching the primary stack.
Active AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks, satisfying the AI agent development requirement explicitly.
Demonstrated testing culture through TDD advocacy, conference workshops, and open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), aligning with the Vitest/Jest/Playwright quality bar.
concerns: No explicit NestJS experience mentioned anywhere in the resume, despite it being a named primary framework in the stack.
Kotlin/Java appears only in older embedded/robotics contexts (2016–2018); no recent JVM backend work to substantiate the secondary-technology requirement.
Current role (re:cinq, April 2025–present) is very recent with limited demonstrated output; depth of current-stack experience is hard to verify from the resume alone.
response.justification: Bogdan is a strong technical match on depth and breadth: TypeScript/Node.js/React runs throughout his career, his cloud and containerization exposure is genuine, and his current AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq directly addresses a differentiating requirement of this role. The main gap is NestJS — a named primary framework that never appears in the resume — and a thin recent Kotlin/Java footprint, both of which carry some onboarding risk but are unlikely to be blockers given his demonstrated ability to pick up new frameworks across many stacks.
